summary: Iota Iota Iota (Triota): an honor society dedicated to the ideals of excellence in women's studies scholarship; promotes an interest in research, community service, and social issues

summary: PPAS is an organization focused on adequately providing students with the resources and knowledge they need to successfully apply and matriculate into Physician Assistant School after graduation.

summary: UA Habitat for Humanity is a student-run service organization with a mission to bring people together to build homes, communities, and hope. Our vision is a world where everyone has a decent place to live.

summary: We are Alabama Astrobotics, a robotics team that competes each year in the NASA Lunabotics Competition at Kennedy Space Center. We also aim to increase STEM awareness in the community with various outreach events.

summary: The purpose of the Association shall be to introduce and to promote interest in all aspects of life and culture of Bangladesh and to serve the current and prospective students from Bangladesh studying at UA and to meet their various needs.

summary: This organization provides support and opportunities (including travel and research funding) for members in the English graduate program.

name: Upsilon Pi Epsilon
summary: An honor society for outstanding undergraduate and graduate students in Computer Science.

name: Alpha Phi Sigma
summary: The goal of Alpha Phi Sigma is to recognize and promote high academic achievement among actively participating members and to create a competitive atmosphere for further advancement of criminal justice students.

name: American Concrete Institute
summary: A non-profit technical and educational society. ACI is a forum for the discussion of all matters related to concrete and the development of solutions to problems.

name: The UA Club Golf Team
summary: The UA Club Golf Team's mission is to provide non-varsity golfers the opportunity to continue their playing career in a competitive and friendly atmosphere...(click read more)

name: Radio Television Digital News Association Capstone Chapter
summary: RTDNA is dedicated to setting standards for newsgathering and reporting. Although news techniques and technologies are constantly changing, RTDNA's commitment to encouraging excellence in the electronic journalism industry remains the same.

name: Bryan K. Fair Chapter of the Black Law Students Association
summary: BLSA is an organization designed to support and promote diversity within the law school and legal profession.

name: Professional Women in Communication and Business
summary: PWCB is an organization devoted to developing successful leaders on campus and in the workplace. PWCB helps to promote professional development and gives members a chance to network with other like-minded, motivated individuals.

name: Avanti Alumni Network
summary: The Avanti Alumni Network is meant to connect former University of Alabama Avantis and build a strong network of alumni to help with professional networking, team recruitment and maintaining teammate relationships! After all, you're an Avanti for LIFE!

name: Rising Tide Film Festival (formerly Black Warrior)
summary: The Rising Tide Film Festival strives to provide an outlet for students to showcase their films, as well as to create a film festival environment for The University of Alabama. We showcase weekly student film screenings and monthly social events.

name: Tuska UAV Design Team
summary: We are an interdisciplinary team at the University of Alabama developing unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV's)  for disaster relief, photogrammetry, and other experimental applications.

name: Alabama Rocketry Association
summary: The Alabama Rocketry Association (ARA) is the University of Alabama's rocket team dedicated to providing world-class collegiate engineering experiences for students by competing in national and international rocketry competitions and representing UA!

name: University Programs
summary: University Programs fosters community and maximizes student development through campus-wide events implemented by UA students.

name: Transfer Law Students Association
summary: The purpose of the TLSA is to welcome transfer, visiting and international law students to the UA School of Law and support them in their transition to the law school. The TLSA will serve as a network for information, friendship, and mentoring.

name: Discovery Buddies
summary: Discovery Buddies is a mentoring program which sparks children's interest in science and their own curiosity about the world. Elementary and University of Alabama students work together to perform fun science experiments in a weekly after school program.

name: Alabama Student Rural Health Association
summary: To educate students and Alabamians regarding rural health issues in the state by addressing pertinent health issues through activities, workshops, volunteering, and information distribution.

name: OutLaw
summary: OutLaw is an organization of lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, intersex, asexual, and ally members of the University of Alabama Law School community.

name: Veterans in Business
summary: Veterans in Business provides veterans and service members a business professional community that provides resources, mentorship, networking opportunities, and professional development while at The University of Alabama.

name: Crimson Smash Club
summary: Crimson Smash Club is your premier source for competitive Super Smash Bros. at the University of Alabama! Whether you're a seasoned pro or want to learn a unique and fast-paced fighting game, join us for weekly gaming sessions and monthly tournaments.

name: Kappa Kappa Psi: National Honorary Band Fraternity
summary: Kappa Kappa Psi operates primarily as a student service and leadership recognition society whose chief aim is to assist the Director of Bands in developing the leadership and enthusiasm that is required of his/her band.

name: Graduate Social Work Organization
summary: The purpose of this graduate organization is advocate, promote, and research social injustice issues that affect individuals in the surrounding communities that are oppressed and vulnerable to help meet the needs of the individuals in the community.

name: First Year Experience
summary: First Year Experience (FYE) is an area of Student Life committed to supporting academic and social transition to The University of Alabama. We define first-year student as any undergraduate student attending UA and transfer students.

name: Alabama Chem-E Car Team
summary: The members of this club must design, build, and test a miniature car that starts and stops solely on chemical reactions.  The car competes in a regional competition each spring, in hopes of qualifying for the national competition each fall.

name: The University of Alabama EcoCAR Team
summary: The University of Alabama is competing in the EcoCAR EV Challenge, a competition organized by Argonne National Lab and sponsored by The U.S. Department of Energy, MathWorks, and General Motors to redesign and optimize an electric production vehicle.

name: Ducks Unlimited Chapter at The University of Alabama
summary: The Crimson Tide Chapter of Ducks Unlimited is one of the elite collegiate chapters of ducks unlimited ranking with in the top ten of all Ducks Unlimited Chapters. We hope to help the national organization of ducks unlimited to preserve ducks for others.

name: Biology Graduate Student Association
summary: BGSA serves to represent the graduate students in the UA Department of Biological Sciences. We work to foster community, support one another through graduate school, and to bring graduate student issues to the attention of the faculty when needed.

name: Students United with America's Toothfairy
summary: Students United with America's Toothfairy is an organization aimed to educate and introduce the importance of oral health to children in undeserved communities through fun and interactive science presentations.

name: The University of Alabama Student Center
summary: The Ferguson Student Center, affectionately known as “the Ferg”, is the heart of campus. From grabbing coffee or a meal to providing space to hang out and relax with friends, you can find a place for most anything at the Ferg.

name: Vote Everywhere
summary: Vote Everywhere is a nonpartisan, experiential program that recruits, trains and supports emerging leaders in America’s universities. Our students strengthen democracy by leading long-term voter engagement, public policy and social justice initiatives.

name: Vertical Flight Society - UA Chapter
summary: VFS aims to foster and advance research, innovation, and professional development in vertical flight technology. This is accomplished by student-led projects and educational initiatives focused on rotorcraft, eVTOL, and related aerodynamic applications.

name: Society for Human Resource Management Student Chapter
summary: We provide students with the opportunity to gain knowledge and insight into the effective management of human capital in the field of Human Resource Management through affiliation with the Tuscaloosa HR Professionals and the SHRM organization.

name: UA Young Americans for Freedom Chapter
summary: The UA Young American’s for Freedom chapter is an organization designed to provide conservative students the opportunity to both network within the conservative movement, as well as to serve as a platform of which to advance conservative principles.

name: The Writers Guild
summary: The University of Alabama Writers Guild cultivates an undergraduate creative writing community and provides fun and enriching opportunities for members to grow artistically while being part of a sustaining fellowship.

name: Bama Tutors for Service
summary: Bama Tutors for Service is an entirely student-run volunteer organization that provides free tutoring to local students. We tutor multiple times per week at the Tuscaloosa Public Library.

name: Black Faculty and Staff Association Ambassadors
summary: The objective and purpose of Black Faculty and Staff AmbassadorsBFSAA) is primarily to enhance the relationship between the Black Faculty and Staff Association and the African-American student body.

name: The B+ Foundation at Alabama
summary: The Andrew McDonough B+ Foundation at The University of Alabama is dedicated to “Kids Helping Kids Fight Cancer." Our primary objective is to raise funds to provide both financial & emotional support to families with children fighting cancer

name: Communication Studies Club
summary: An org. dedicated to promoting interest in communication studies. Doing so by providing resources to members, encouraging the study of communication, hosting social events, and helping members gain experience through partnership with other student orgs.

name: Asian American Student Association
summary: A social organization where students can come together and have an open dialogue about Asian American cultures and experiences.

name: Alabama Undergraduate Research Association
summary: The purpose of this association is to promote a community for students who are interested in, have conducted, or are currently involved in undergraduate research at the University of Alabama.

name: Legal Research Club
summary: The Legal Research Club is dedicated to helping Pre-Law students by connecting them with the resources they need to be successful in the areas of legal research and writing, especially through the publication of an Undergraduate Law Review.

name: Investment Banking Academy
summary: The Investment Banking Academy seeks to prepare elite students for internships in the financial services industry and to establish The University of Alabama as a premier recruiting destination for banks across the country.

name: Friends of MSF
summary: Our organization will help create spaces in which people can learn about, support, and advocate for the work of MSF-USA, through a variety of events and actions including, without limitation, meetings, film-screenings, fundraising efforts, etc.

name: Alabama Student Genetics Association
summary: Our goal is to educate members and the campus community about genetics. We host informal events, foster a welcoming environment to encourage further learning, and strive to understand the world of genetics better.

name: Association for Women in Mathematics Student Chapter
summary: The Chapter promotes an increased knowledge of and greater interest in the mathematical sciences by mentoring and encouraging women and girls as they prepare for careers in the field.

name: Data Analytics Club
summary: The Data Analytics Club intends to bring together students who aspire to become a data analysts or are pursuing a career related to analytics.

name: Sigma Kappa
summary: The purpose of Sigma Kappa is to provide women lifelong opportunities and support for social, intellectual and spiritual development by bringing women together to positively impact our communities.

name: Manderson Specialized Masters Association
summary: An organization dedicated to the empowerment of specialized masters students enrolled in the Manderson Graduate School of Business through forward-looking development and personal growth.

name: College of Education Graduate Student Organization
summary: The College of Education Graduate Student Organization (COE-GSO) is a student-run organization aimed at representing the broad interests of graduate students in the College of Education at the University of Alabama.

name: Crimson Historical Review
summary: The Crimson Historical Review is the University of Alabama’s first undergraduate history journal, and one of the few of its type in the country. The Editorial Board, Design Team, and Review Board are all comprised of UA undergraduate students.

name: Nano Science and Engineering Association
summary: This organization promotes nanoscience at UA by hosting inter-departmental discussions to promote University of Alabama collaboration and publicly promoting scientific activities to show the connection between science and life.

name: UASPACE
summary: The primary goal of UASPACE is the design, fabrication, testing, launch, orbital insertion, tracking, and successful operation of CubeSats. UASPACE also aims to work as a space advocacy group to the public, especially to K-12 students.

name: MIS Ambassadors
summary: Group of students that serve as the official representatives for the UA MIS program. Ambassadors give tours to prospective MIS students and lead initiatives that promote student success and confidence within the program.

name: First Generation Law Students Association
summary: FGLSA supports first generation law students. Our goal is to assist members in navigating law school by advocating for support through relevant professional, social, and academic opportunities within and outside the law school community.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
summary: SHPE changes lives by empowering the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development.

name: Black Business Student Association
summary: The purpose of the Black Business Student Association is to foster a sense of community for Black-identifying students in the Culverhouse College of Business at the University of Alabama.

name: Psychology Graduate Student Association
summary: The Psychology Graduate Student Association is a group of elected doctoral students in the psychology department who work to promote graduate student interests and provide opportunities for socializing and professional development for graduate students.

name: Physics and Astronomy Graduate Student Association
summary: PAGSA is an organization for physics students at The University of Alabama. The benefits of PAGSA public events are intended for the general public, including The University of Alabama students.

name: Management Doctoral Student Organization
summary: The MDSO is a student-run organization for students in the PhD program in the Management Department at the University of Alabama.

name: Mentor Undergraduate Peer Partnering
summary:
Mentor UPP is a mentoring and networking program designed to cultivate relationships
between upperclassmen and underclassmen to increase involvement, retention, and confidence in the newest members of the College of Engineering.

name: Young Water Professionals
summary: We are an interdisciplinary professional society for students of any major who are interested in a career in the field of water and/or wastewater.

name: National Retail Federation Student Association
summary: The NRF Student Association provides talented students interested in retail and business careers unique educational and scholarship programs, networking opportunities and access to the latest industry news and research.

name: Tide Timing Clock Club
summary: Tide Timing Clock Club (T² C²) is a platform for undergraduate and graduate students of STEM at UA. The club actively provides hands-on experience in Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT). Teaching GPS, timescale, & atomic clock development concepts.

name: STEMinists: Graduate Students in STEM
summary: STEMinists is an organization for STEM graduate students of all identities who are committed to inclusivity, social outreach, networking, mentoring, and building community at UA

name: STEM and CREATE Path to the MBA Ambassadors
summary: The STEM and CREATE Path to the MBA Ambassadors promote the STEM and CREATE Path to the MBA program to current students at UA as well as people and organizations outside of the university.

name: Crimson Creatives
summary: We’re a community of creatives, deep thinkers, and media appreciators who come together to share original work, spark meaningful conversations, and explore the constantly evolving world of Music, Film, and Tv.

name: Housing and Residential Communities
summary: Living on campus is the key to a great start at The University of Alabama. Residential living puts you at the heart of campus life, where you will interact with a diverse community of people.

name: University Recreation
summary: University Recreation’s facilities and programs provide the perfect places to get moving and keep an active lifestyle.

name: Student Care and Well-Being
summary: The Student Care and Well-Being office was established to provide assistance and case management for students in crisis and outreach to parents and families.

name: Student Conduct
summary: The Office of Student Conduct seeks to maximize each UA student’s learning experience by educating students about the Student Code of Conduct, as well as the Capstone Creed.

name: Veteran and Military Affairs
summary: The Office for Veteran and Military Affairs provides, facilitates, and coordinates programs and services to meet the special needs of veterans, service members, dependents, and survivors.

name: UA Homecoming
summary: Homecoming engages The University of Alabama community in a cohesive celebration of the Capstone’s rich traditions.

name: Assessment and Planning
summary: Creating a data driven culture for informed decision making within the Division of Student Life

name: Student Advocates for Foster Families
summary: The purpose of this association is to educate others on the foster care system and needs of foster families, while providing opportunities for students to serve foster families.

name: Masters of Public Administration Student Association
summary: The purpose of this association is to extend the Masters of Public Administration (MPA) program’s mission of preparing MPA graduate and undergraduate Accelerated Master’s Program (AMP) students to lead and manage public service organizations.

name: Masters in Management Information Systems
summary: MMIS serves all MIS Masters students to build stronger personal and professional relationships through community events and professional development programming.

name: Student Life Communications
summary: Student Life Communications

name: The Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers
summary: SASE UA exists to prepare the growing Asian population at the University of Alabama for the professional world, to promote inclusivity and tolerance on campus, and to give back to the local community.

name: Historical Martial Arts Organization
summary: The core was to research and study from ancient resources, restore and practice martial arts with safety equipments.

name: Graduate Society of Women Engineers
summary: GradSWE is a part of the larger Society of Women Engineers (SWE) organization. SWE, founded in 1950, is a not-for-profit educational and service organization that empowers women to succeed in STEM (or STEAM) careers.

name: Business Law Society
summary: The purposes of BLS are to present educational public discussion groups, forums, panels, or lectures relating to business law in efforts to elicit insights and broaden the perspectives of student members before graduation.

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America at the University of Alabama
summary: UA chapter of the national nonprofit ALPFA. We provide professional development workshops and career resources to our members to empower and develop leaders for the nation. Our members access scholarships, career fairs, networking events and mentorship.

name: The OM Collective
summary: The OM Collective is an organization dedicated to professional, personal, and social development in the major of Operations Management. Our main goal is to empower our fellow students who will be entering the operations management industry.

name: Women in Physics and Astronomy
summary: We are a club celebrating women and gender minorities in physics and astronomy with equal parts social and career development meetings and events!

name: Lee J. Styslinger Jr. College of Engineering Ambassadors
summary: The Ambassadors of the Styslinger College of Engineering use their passion and enthusiasm for engineering to serve as the official representatives of the College of Engineering. They function as hosts and hostesses for events and prospective students.

name: The Accounting Masters Association
summary: Masters of Accountancy and Tax students.

name: The Higher Education Assembly of Research in Tuscaloosa
summary: The Higher Education Assembly of Research in Tuscaloosa (HEART) is to serve as a source of social, emotional, and professional support for minority students involved in research and to actively promote the interests of these individuals

name: Tide Talks Wealth
summary: Tide Talks Wealth is an all-women's finance organization that provides training, resources, and mentorship to help members find their career path through a more holistic understanding of the industry.

name: Will Nolan Film Club
summary: The Will Nolan Film Club hosts viewings and discussions of notable films in order to grow the understanding of cinema and continue on with the late instructor, Will Nolan.

name: The Journal of the Legal Profession
summary: The Journal of the Legal Profession is the nation's oldest periodical exploring the dynamic nature of the legal profession. The Journal is student-run by the Managing Board and Junior Editors.

name: Student Life Business Services
summary: This is the departmental MySource page for the Student Life Business Services team.

name: SmartPay Society
summary: SmartPay Society is The University of Alabama’s first Fintech (Financial Technology) Club which’s primary mission is to educate members and the community about financial technology trends, innovations and opportunities.

name: The Joy Project
summary: The Joy Project is a student-led volunteer group bringing joy to underserved children in Tuscaloosa. Through events like our annual holiday festival and Letters for Joy pen pal program, we provide gifts, mentorship, and meaningful connections.

name: The American Civic Republicans
summary: The American Civic Republicans is an Alabama Law student organization dedicated to American Founding Principles and the widespread inculcation of civic virtue as envisioned by various Founding Fathers, most notably George Washington.

name: CrossingPoints Program
summary: CrossingPoints is a multi-tiered postsecondary transition program in the College of Education at The University of Alabama and serves college-age students with intellectual disabilities.

name: Electrical & Computer Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: Association for the graduate students in the ECE department

name: First Gen+ Programs
summary: First Gen+ Programs supports students who are the first in their family to attend college or students who may experience barriers such as financial need through community building, mentorship, and personal and professional development.

name: Economics Student Association
summary: The ESA is an organization designed to provide a source of community for students with an interest in economics.

name: The Crimson K-9s
summary: We are committed to fostering an inclusive community of dog owners! Join us for bi-weekly meetings where we come together to build a strong sense of community!

name: Education Abroad
summary: The Education Abroad office assists students in pursuing educational opportunities all over the globe. Come ask us how you can study, intern, research, teach, or volunteer abroad. It's time to discover the world!

name: Black In Medicine
summary: BIM is a hands-on organization. We want our members to gain valuable experience that will have a long-term impact on their careers. Our goal is to ensure that you have experience, knowledge, and an in-depth understanding of the healthcare field.

name: Ghana Diaspora Network
summary: The Ghana Diaspora Network at the University of Alabama is dedicated to enriching the cultural tapestry within the university community while forging connections between Ghanaian students and African Americans with roots in Ghana.

name: Society of Experimental and Computational Mechanics
summary: The purpose of this organization is to give graduate students in the field of Engineering who are involved in experimental and computational research a platform to present their work in front of their peers,

name: Student Affiliates in School Psychology
summary: Student Affiliates in School Psychology aims to support graduate students in the school psychology program with activities that promote professional growth and cultivate community among SASP members.

name: Crimson Records
summary: Mock record label, facilitating collaboration, performances, and marketing between aspiring musical artists.

name: Red Rook Press
summary: Red Rook Press is a student-run publishing house operating out of UA that publishes novel-length work from undergraduate and graduate students across the country. 50+ members work all year to help publish 2-3 books at the end of every spring semester.

name: The University of Alabama Museums
summary: A combined account for all of The University of Alabama's museums.

name: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine
summary:
The National Society of Black Women in Medicine is a multi-disciplined organization that works to
increase the recruitment and retention of Black women pursuing broad careers in medicine, while simultaneously promoting the advancement of those already e

name: American Library Association at UA
summary: The American Library Association at UA works to promote scholarship and professional ethics, provide professional contacts within the field, and increase awareness in the American Library Association.

name: National Kitchen and Bath Association Student Chapter
summary: Connected with the National Kitchen and Bath Association, this organization looks to deepen interior students connections in the design world and to help them further explore the specialty of kitchen and bath design.

name: Whitfield Patient Experience Program
summary: The Whitfield Patient Experience Program aims to provides students both with exposure to the challenges of rural healthcare and a setting to share their experiences while learning about the Demopolis community.

name: Association for Information Systems
summary: AIS exists to promote the opportunities and knowledge of career opinions within the IT field through professional development, social networking, and community development.

name: Alabama Leadership Association
summary: At the Alabama Leadership Association, we are dedicated to cultivating the leaders of tomorrow through a commitment to leadership development and impactful community service.

name: Building Our Successful Sisters
summary: Building Our Successful Sisters is a professional development organization dedicated to the advancement of minority collegiate women in professional spaces while also emphasizing the importance of networking, community service, and academic success.

name: Latinx Law Student Association
summary: The Latinx Law Students Association ("LLSA") at the University of Alabama School of Law is dedicated to advancing the voices of Latin American law students, promoting academic success, and fostering a commitment to our community.

name: Association for Computing Machinery - Women
summary: ACM-W is an organization dedicated to fostering a supportive community for women pursuing studies and careers in computer science and related fields. We provide opportunities for women to connect, collaborate, and support each other in their endeavors.

name: College of Arts and Sciences Dean's Office
summary: Events, workshops, and conferences for students hosted by The College of Arts and Sciences.

name: The Undergraduate Student Council of Chemical and Biological Engineering
summary: The USC is a voice for the student body in meetings and other communications with the faculty and staff of the ChBE department and will reflect the interests of the ChBE student body consistent with the purpose and stated scope of the USC.

name: The Psychology Research Think Tank
summary: An undergraduate focused research club working with Dr. Darren George, with the goal to brainstorm, conduct and publish the highest quality psychology research with student authors and co-authors, as well as to connect students together through research.

name: Minervites
summary: An organization dedicated to supporting students in Minerva, the Creative Advertising Program within the College of Communication and Information Sciences at The University of Alabama.

name: ASCE - Steel Bridge Team
summary: ASCE Steel Bridge is a student competition with the objective of designing and constructing a steel bridge in order to compete in the ASCE Gulf Coast Student Symposium.

name: Biotech and Bioethics Association
summary: The Biotech and Bioethics Association (BaBA) aims to provide students with a platform to discuss topics in biotechnology and their bioethical implications, as well as inform members of opportunities for hands-on involvement in biotech and bioethics.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha Political Science Honor Society
summary: The purpose of the organization is to recognize excellence in academic achievement in the fields of political science, to stimulate scholarship and interest and promote worthwhile curricular and extracurricular activities related to political science.

name: Capstone Asset Management Society
summary: The Capstone Asset Management Society (CAMS) is a student-led organization dedicated to helping members grow their knowledge of investing and prepare for successful careers in finance.

name: Graduate Association of Civil, Construction, and Environmental Engineering
summary: GRACCEE is a graduate student organization which aims to connect students within the Civil, Construction, and Environmental Engineering Department. Various professional development opportunities as well as social gatherings will be held.

name: College of Education FIRE: First-Generation Initiatives for Research and Education
summary: The purpose of College of Education FIRE is to empower first-generation graduate students to navigate graduate school, thrive in their program, and build community across peers.

name: Black Aspiring Scholars in Education & Exercise Science
summary: The purpose of B.A.S.E. is to advocate for a fair and inclusive educational experience with an emphasis on African American and minority students' growth and development through the Capstone.

name: Weeks of Welcome
summary: UA’s Weeks of Welcome (WOW) is a large series of programs coordinated by University Programs and the Division of Student Life to help all students find resources, build community and have fun.

name: OPTICA at The University of Alabama
summary: Optica, Advancing Optics and Photonics Worldwide, is the society dedicated to promoting the generation, application, archiving and dissemination of knowledge in the field.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law & Government Fraternity - Alpha Tau Chapter
summary: Kappa Alpha Pi exists for undergraduate students interested in law and government and is open to all undergraduate focuses of study. We are devoted to developing students professionally and academically to be leaders in the world in law and government.

name: Pakistani Student Association
summary: The Pakistani Student Association at the UA provides a welcoming space for Pakistani students, sharing cultural traditions and promoting unity. Through events and gatherings, PSA fosters support, friendship, and cultural exchange within the UA community.

name: Association for Women Geoscientists at the University of Alabama
summary: AWG at UA aims to enhance the quality and level of participation of women in geosciences and to encourage girls and young women (including cis-gender, transgender, gender fluid and non-binary persons) to explore geoscience careers.

name: The Immunology Club
summary: A club dedicated to providing students at the University of Alabama with faculty and graduate student led research presentations about topics in immunology.

name: Alabama Diplomacy Club
summary: The Alabama Diplomacy Club is club all about the board game Diplomacy! We host in-person and online games and play variants and other similar games. We hope to grow the Diplomacy hobby community and spread a love of the game to UA and Tuscaloosa.

name: Venture Capital Hub
summary: The Venture Capital Hub is an environment that gives students practical experience when it comes to all things venture capital, including investor decision-making and startup dynamics.

name: Institute for Social Science Research
summary: The Institute for Social Science Research (ISSR) at The University of Alabama is a hub for interdisciplinary research, collaboration, and innovation in the social sciences.

name: Alabama Engineering Initiative
summary: The Alabama Engineering Initiative (AEI) is a student-led organization at The University of Alabama that partners with universities and humanitarian groups to design sustainable engineering solutions and develop community-focused engineers.

name: Holle Center for Communication Arts
summary: The Holle Center for Communication Arts is an incubator and a creative collaboratory designed to shape the future of 21st-century nonfiction storytelling.

name: Girls Who Lead (UA Chapter)
summary: Girls Who Lead (UA Chapter) is an organization dedicated to pushing college women into policy, advocacy, and entrepreneurship, all while closing the opportunity gap to help girls rise into positions of leadership at the University of Alabama and beyond!

name: 57 Miles
summary: 57 Miles aims to build sustainable relationships between The University of Alabama and the community of Marion, Alabama, through service, education, and engagement.

name: Unite for Reproductive & Gender Equity
summary: URGE envisions a liberated world where we can live with justice, love freely, express our gender and sexuality, and define and create families of our choosing.

name: Crimson Sword Fighting
summary: Our purpose is to give students the opportunity to study historical martial techniques developed by European sword masters in the Middle Ages.

name: Bitcoin Alabama
summary: Educational and Social club discussing bitcoin.

name: Office of Undergraduate Research
summary: The Office for Undergraduate Research is dedicated to enriching the undergraduate experience through hands-on research opportunities.

name: Student Tide Against D.U.I
summary: My organization is dedicated to raising awareness about the dangers of drunk driving and promoting safer ways for students to get home. We aim to create a designated driver program at UA events, support MADD through fundraising, and work with campus orgs

name: Black with a Small Business
summary: Foster relationships with students at the University of Alabama to build a community of present and future entrepreneurs.

name: Hispanic Latino Pre Health Organization
summary: The purpose of the Hispanic Latino Pre-Health Organization (HLPHO) is to empower, support, and equip Hispanic and Latino students pursuing careers in health-related fields.

name: Architectural Engineering Institute
summary: The Architectural Engineering Institute (AEI) is the official organization for students studying Architectural Engineering, who plan to gain experience with working with both architects and civil engineers in their respective career fields.

name: Blockchain at UA
summary: Our mission is to build a community of builders and analysts preparing for opportunities in the growing blockchain ecosystem.

name: Welcome Back Weeks
summary: Welcome Back Weeks is the official kick off each spring semester to welcome all students back to the Capstone. Coordinated by University Programs, this large series of programs are free and open to all UA students.

name: Morgan's Message at UA
summary: Morgan's Message at UA is a campus chapter of the international organization, Morgan's Message which serves to eliminate the stigma surrounding student-athlete mental health.

name: Capstone Consulting Society
summary: The Capstone Consulting Society prepares University of Alabama students for success in consulting and related business, analytical, and leadership fields.  CCS provides an inclusive environment for students from all majors to build technical proficiency.

name: The Alexander Hamilton Society of the University of Alabama
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society of the University of Alabama was established to provide UA students with programming relevant to U.S. foreign, economic, and national security policy.

name: ChemE Jeopardy Team
summary: The ChemE Jeopardy Team is a competition team that participates in the annual ChemE Jeopardy regional and national competitions. The team serves to promote competition, academic excellence, and community among members.

name: Middle Eastern and Asian Law Student Society
summary: Foster community among Middle Eastern and Asian law students while promoting awareness of legal issues affecting these communities and creating professional development opportunities. Will have social events, academic and professional development events.

name: ColorStack at The University of Alabama
summary: ColorStack's mission is to increase the number of Black and Latinx Computer Science graduates that go on to start rewarding technical careers.

name: Styslinger College of Engineering
summary: The Lee J. Styslinger Jr. College of Engineering offers many activities students can pursue while at the Capstone. Our programs are designed so our students can meet their personal education goals while also allowing them to experience life fully.

name: Students for Epilepsy Support and Education
summary: SESE is a student-led organization focused on building a supportive community for students living with epilepsy and those affected by it, while raising awareness, advocating for accessibility, and promoting education through campus collaboration.

name: Pokémon Club
summary: The Pokémon club is a community for students who enjoy Pokémon in any form, including video games, trading cards, anime, manga, and related media.

name: GPA Rescue
summary: The purpose of GPA Rescue is to support the academic success and personal development of  students at The University of Alabama by providing peer-based academic assistance and  mentorship.

name: Chem-E Cube at UA
summary: The purpose of Chem-E Cube is to design, build, and test a functional chemical engineering system constrained to one cubic foot (1 ft³).
